essence: Transforms simple ingredients into breads, pastries, and baked goods through precision, timing, and craft.

identity.title: Baker
identity.summary: Prepares and bakes breads, pastries, cakes, and other goods using recipes, techniques, and careful temperature control.

salary.notes: Artisan bakers, patisserie specialists, and bakery owners can exceed £40k–£60k+ depending on skill, niche, and business scale.

qualifications: Apprenticeship or Diploma in Baking or Patisserie
Knowledge of Doughs, Pastry, and Baking Techniques
Understanding of Food Safety and Hygiene Standards
Experience with Ovens, Mixers, and Bakery Equipment

lifestyle.description: Bakers work in warm, fast‑paced environments where early mornings, precision, and consistency are essential. Days often start before sunrise, involving mixing doughs, shaping loaves, preparing pastries, monitoring ovens, and ensuring products are fresh for customers. The work is physical and repetitive but deeply satisfying — each batch is a blend of technique, timing, and craft. Bakers often work in bakeries, patisseries, supermarkets, or artisan shops. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy hands‑on work, routine, creativity with ingredients, and the joy of producing food that brings comfort and delight.
